title: APIClient service (auth + base request handling)
description: Create mac/Sources/Services/APIClient.swift. Handles: base URL config, auth token injection (from Keychain via auth pattern inherited from audiotake2), async request/response with Codable, error handling mapped to PabloAPIError. Foundation for all backend calls.

title: Epic 6: Therapist Onboarding
[epic] Epic 9: Windows Session UI
Day-view screen (WinUI 3 XAML — list of today's sessions)
Session row component + Start Session button
Session detail panel
Pablo Bear empty state (no sessions today)
Day-view screen (list of today's sessions)
description: Build the main SessionListView showing today's scheduled sessions from the Pablo backend. Uses SessionListViewModel with @Published sessions array. Shows session time, patient name, status badge, and Start Session CTA. Today's sessions are the hero UI element.

title: New session / new patient creation flow
description: Sheet or navigation flow to create a new session or patient. Minimum fields: patient name, session time, video platform. POSTs to Pablo backend via APIClient. Accessible from the day view when sessions are empty or via + button.

title: 'Start Session' button — triggers recording + video launch
description: Wire the Start Session button to: (1) call RecordingService.startRecording(), (2) call VideoLaunchService.launch(for: session). Transitions session status to in-progress. Handles the case where video platform is not configured (prompt to set up).

title: Pass Firebase API key as HTTP header instead of URL query parameter
description:
## Problem
TokenRefresher.swift embeds the Firebase API key directly in the URL query string:
```swift
let urlString = "https://securetoken.googleapis.com/v1/token?key=\(apiKey)"
```
Query parameters appear in server access logs, proxy logs, and browser history.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Firebase API key is passed as an HTTP header (e.g. `X-Goog-Api-Key` or per Firebase docs)
- URL no longer contains the key as a query parameter
- Verify Firebase token endpoint accepts key via header (check Firebase REST API docs)

## Files
- `mac/PabloCompanion/Services/TokenRefresher.swift` (line 43)

title: Validate auth server URL input: reject non-HTTPS and unknown hosts
description:
## Problem
The auth server URL in LoginView/SettingsView is a free-form text field with no validation. A user (or attacker with physical access) could point the app at `http://attacker.com`, causing credentials to be sent to a malicious server.

## Acceptance Criteria
- URL scheme must be `https` (or `http` only in DEBUG builds for localhost)
- Show inline validation error if user enters an invalid URL
- `buildAuthURL()` in AuthViewModel throws / returns nil for invalid URLs — never silently proceeds
- Consider maintaining an allowlist of known Pablo auth hosts

## Files
- `mac/PabloCompanion/ViewModels/AuthViewModel.swift` (buildAuthURL)
- `mac/PabloCompanion/Views/LoginView.swift` (auth server TextField)
- `mac/PabloCompanion/Views/SettingsView.swift`

title: Fix logger subsystem name and Keychain service name to use actual bundle ID
description:
## Problem
Two stale identifiers from early development remain in the codebase:

1. **Logger subsystem**: `com.macos-sample` used across APIClient, AuthViewModel, and others. HIPAA audit log filtering requires the correct subsystem.
2. **Keychain service name**: `com.therapyrecorder.auth` in KeychainManager.swift. Should reflect the actual bundle identifier to avoid namespace collisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All `Logger(subsystem:)` calls use the actual app bundle identifier (derive via `Bundle.main.bundleIdentifier ?? "com.pablo.companion"`)
- `KeychainManager.serviceName` updated to match bundle identifier
- No functional regression in token storage/retrieval

## Files
- `mac/PabloCompanion/Services/APIClient.swift`
- `mac/PabloCompanion/Services/KeychainManager.swift`
- `mac/PabloCompanion/ViewModels/AuthViewModel.swift`
- Any other file using `Logger(subsystem: "com.macos-sample"...)`

title: Wire deleteAfterUpload toggle to actual post-upload file deletion in UploadViewModel
description:
The 'Delete recording from device after upload' toggle (@AppStorage('deleteAfterUpload')) was added to SettingsView in PABLO-D-96 but is not yet connected to any deletion logic.

## What needs to be done
In UploadViewModel (or wherever upload completion is handled), after a successful upload:
1. Read the deleteAfterUpload @AppStorage value
2. If true, delete the local recording file from disk
3. Update the local recording state accordingly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] After successful upload, recording file deleted from disk if toggle is ON
- [ ] If toggle is OFF, file is retained (as expected)
- [ ] Deletion failure is handled gracefully (log error, don't crash)
- [ ] No encrypted file data left on disk after deletion

title: Epic: Transcription Phase 1 — Local 1:1 MVP
description: Local on-device transcription for 1:1 therapy sessions. Two Whisper passes (mic → THERAPIST, system → CLIENT), merge by timestamp, canonical JSON, Google Meet format export. Zero diarization required in 1:1 mode. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md

title: Bootstrap pablo-core Rust crate (Cargo.toml, UniFFI, Xcode linking)
description: Create core/Cargo.toml, core/src/lib.rs, core/uniffi/pablo_core.udl. Wire UniFFI build script. Add generated Swift bindings to Xcode target. Update Makefile build-core and test-core targets to actually run. Verify dylib links correctly in a macOS Debug build. This is the foundation all other pablo-core tasks depend on. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §9 (Where Code Lives)

title: Update RecordingService for AudioCaptureKit separateFiles mode
description: Update RecordingService.swift to use CaptureConfiguration(streamMode: .separateFiles). Update LocalRecording model to add systemAudioFileURL: URL? and systemAudioChecksum: String?. Update stopRecording() to populate both fields from RecordingResult. Update RecordingListView and any other consumers. Note: blocked on AudioCaptureKit separate streams implementation (separate repo — see docs/audiocapturekit-separate-streams-design.md). Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §3 Recording

title: Rust core: audio preprocessing (resample + mono downmix for Whisper)
description: In pablo-core: accept a 48kHz WAV file path, resample to 16kHz mono PCM f32, return as Vec<f32> ready for whisper-rs. Use 'rubato' crate for high-quality resampling. For system audio (stereo): downmix L+R to mono before resampling. Expose via UniFFI as an async fn. Must handle both mono (mic) and stereo (system) input. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §4 (Key parameters: 16kHz mono PCM)

title: Rust core: Whisper integration via whisper-rs + model loading
description: Add whisper-rs v0.15.1 dependency to pablo-core. Implement WhisperTranscriber struct: loads GGML model from path, exposes transcribe(audio: Vec<f32>) -> Vec<RawSegment>. Support quality presets (Fast=small, Balanced=large-v3-turbo Q5_0, HighAccuracy=large-v3). Params: language=en, word_timestamps=true, beam_size=5. Expose model_path configuration via UniFFI. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §4 ASR

title: Rust core: 1:1 transcription pipeline (two Whisper passes → canonical JSON)
description: Implement the 1:1 session pipeline in pablo-core: (1) preprocess + transcribe mic file, label all segments THERAPIST; (2) preprocess + transcribe system audio file, label all segments CLIENT; (3) merge both segment lists sorted by start_time; (4) produce TranscriptResult (canonical JSON schema). Handle the case where systemAudioFileURL is nil (mic-only recording). Expose as async fn transcribe_session_1on1(mic_path, system_path, config) -> TranscriptResult via UniFFI. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §4 + §6 Canonical Schema

title: Rust core: Google Meet format renderer
description: Render a TranscriptResult to the exact Google Meet plain-text format expected by the downstream Pablo SOAP note pipeline. Format confirmed from synthetic session data in meeting-transcription repo. Rules: header (Google Meet Transcript / Session Date / Duration), turns as [HH:MM:SS] / Speaker Name: text, blank lines between turns, [Session ends HH:MM:SS], --- separator, speaker list with roles. Merge adjacent same-speaker segments (gap ≤1.5s). New turn on speaker change or pause >3s. Expose as fn render_google_meet(transcript: TranscriptResult) -> String via UniFFI. Full format spec: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §6 Google Meet Format Export

title: Swift: Whisper model bundle + download/cache management
description: Bundle whisper-large-v3-turbo Q5_0 (~1.0 GB) and whisper-small (~200 MB) in the app bundle for Fast and Balanced presets. Store in ~/Library/Application Support/PabloCompanion/Models/. Add ModelManager service: resolves model path for a given QualityPreset, checks bundled vs cached versions. Stub for on-demand download of whisper-large-v3 (High Accuracy) from Pablo CDN in a future task. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §7 Model Management

title: Swift: transcription settings UI (quality preset, session type, auto-transcribe)
description: Add transcription section to SettingsView: (1) Quality Preset picker (Fast / Balanced / High Accuracy) with hardware guardrails — warn if High Accuracy picked on <16GB RAM or Intel Mac; (2) Session Type picker (1:1 / Couples) — 1:1 is default, Couples only shown if user has couples patients; (3) Auto-transcribe toggle (on by default). Detect Apple Silicon vs Intel and available RAM via host_info / sysctl. Store preferences via @AppStorage. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §7 User-Facing Settings

title: Swift: post-session transcription trigger + upload transcript to backend
description: Wire up the local transcription pipeline to run automatically after a session recording stops (when auto-transcribe is on). TranscriptionViewModel: calls pablo-core transcribe_session_1on1(), renders to Google Meet format, then POSTs to POST /api/sessions/{id}/transcript on the Pablo backend (note: backend endpoint must be created separately in the pablo repo). Show transcription progress in RecordingListView. Handle errors gracefully (allow manual retry). Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §10 Phase 1

title: Epic: Transcription Phase 2 — Couples Counseling
description: Speaker diarization for couples sessions (2 remote clients on system audio channel). Silero VAD + ECAPA-TDNN + AHC clustering, lazy-loaded only when needed. 'Name the speakers' UX, persistent client voiceprints keyed by patient ID. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §4 Couples

title: Rust core: Silero VAD + ECAPA-TDNN speaker embeddings (ONNX, lazy-load)
description: Add 'ort' crate (ONNX Runtime) to pablo-core. Implement two lazy-loaded inference components: (1) SileroVad — segments speech from silence on mono 16kHz audio, returns Vec<(f32,f32)> time ranges; (2) EcapaTdnnEmbedder — extracts 192-dim speaker embedding from a speech segment, returns [f32; 192]. Models (Silero VAD ~2MB, ECAPA-TDNN ~20MB ONNX) loaded on first couples session, unloaded after. Use CoreML execution provider on macOS for acceleration. Models sourced from: Silero (github.com/snakers4/silero-vad), WeSpeaker ECAPA-TDNN (ONNX export available). Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §4 Couples — Diarization Components

title: Rust core: AHC clustering + smoothing for 2-speaker diarization
description: Implement the couples diarization pipeline in pablo-core: (1) run Silero VAD on mono system audio to get speech segments; (2) extract ECAPA-TDNN embedding per segment; (3) Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering (AHC) with cosine distance — fixed k=2 clusters (both clients are remote); (4) median filter smoothing to remove rapid switches (<0.3s segments). Expose as fn diarize_two_speaker(audio: Vec<f32>) -> Vec<DiarizedSegment> where DiarizedSegment has {start, end, speaker_id: 0|1}. Pure Rust clustering (no external crate needed for 2-cluster AHC). Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §4 Couples — Diarization Pipeline

title: Rust core: cosine similarity client matching across sessions
description: For returning couples: match new diarized clusters to stored client voiceprints. Compute centroid embedding for each cluster (mean of segment embeddings). For each enrolled client (stored as [f32; 192]): cosine_similarity(centroid, enrolled). Assign cluster to argmax if score > threshold (default 0.30). Return UNKNOWN if no match exceeds threshold — fallback to manual labeling. Expose as fn match_speakers(clusters: Vec<ClusterEmbedding>, enrolled: Vec<EnrolledSpeaker>) -> Vec<SpeakerMatch> via UniFFI. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §4 Matching Algorithm

title: Rust core: couples transcription pipeline (diarize system audio → 3-speaker merge)
description: Extend pablo-core with the couples pipeline variant: (1) transcribe mic → THERAPIST (same as 1:1); (2) transcribe system audio with word timestamps; (3) run diarize_two_speaker on system audio; (4) align Whisper word timestamps to diarization segments to assign CLIENT_A / CLIENT_B; (5) run match_speakers against enrolled voiceprints if available; (6) merge all into TranscriptResult. Expose as async fn transcribe_session_couples(mic_path, system_path, enrolled_speakers, config) -> TranscriptResult via UniFFI. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §4 Couples

title: Swift: enrollment consent dialog for biometric voiceprint collection
description: Show explicit consent dialog before storing any client voiceprint embedding. Required for HIPAA biometric data and state laws (BIPA/Illinois etc.). Dialog must: name what is being collected ('a voice sample to identify speakers in future sessions'), explain storage ('stored encrypted, linked to this patient record'), explain deletion ('deleted when patient record is deleted'), and require explicit Agree / Decline. Store consent timestamp + patient ID in encrypted local DB. Never store voiceprint without recorded consent. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §12 Q5 Enrollment Consent

title: Swift: 'name the speakers' UI — first couples session labeling + voiceprint storage
description: After the first couples session transcript is generated: show a labeling UI with 2 unlabeled speakers. For each: play 2-3 short audio clips from that cluster, show a name input field, let therapist assign each to a patient (picker from patient list). On confirm: (1) show enrollment consent dialog (PABLO-D-105.5); (2) if consented, extract centroid ECAPA-TDNN embedding for each cluster, encrypt, store in local SQLite keyed by patient ID; (3) subsequent couples sessions auto-match — skip the labeling UI and show confidence scores instead. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §4 Enrollment Flow

title: Swift: client voiceprint encrypted local storage (SQLite, keyed by patient ID)
description: Create VoiceprintStore service backed by an encrypted SQLite DB (SQLite.swift or GRDB). Schema: patient_id (text PK), display_name (text), embedding_data (blob — AES-256 encrypted [f32; 192]), consent_recorded_at (datetime), created_at, updated_at. Encrypt embedding using same device key as RecordingEncryptor (from Keychain). Support: store, retrieve by patient ID, delete (called when patient removed), list all. HIPAA: biometric identifier — must be included in data deletion workflows and audit logging. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §4 Embedding Storage + §8 HIPAA

title: Epic: Transcription Phase 3 — Cloud Mode
description: Optional cloud transcription path: encrypted audio upload, job polling, result download. Heavier models (faster-whisper + pyannote) run on backend GPU. System-level toggle. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §5 Cloud Mode

title: Swift: system settings cloud mode toggle
description: Add cloud transcription mode as a system-level setting (fetched from Pablo backend system settings, not per-user). When cloud mode is enabled: SettingsView shows the option, user can switch their processing mode to Cloud. When disabled system-wide: Cloud option is hidden. Add GET /api/settings/transcription client call to fetch the system setting on app launch. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §5 Cloud Mode + §7 Processing Mode setting

title: Swift: encrypted audio upload for cloud transcription jobs
description: When cloud mode is active: after session ends, encrypt mic + system audio files (AES-256, already have RecordingEncryptor), upload both to POST /api/transcription/jobs on Pablo backend. Include session_id, session_type (1on1/couples), quality preset. Show upload progress. Handle upload failure gracefully (retry queue). Note: backend endpoint must be created separately in the pablo repo. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §5 Upload Flow

title: Swift: transcription job polling + result download (cloud mode)
description: Poll GET /api/transcription/jobs/{id} after upload until status is complete or failed. On complete: fetch transcript from GET /api/transcription/jobs/{id}/result (Google Meet format plain text). Store locally and show in transcript viewer. Polling strategy: exponential backoff starting at 5s, cap at 30s, timeout after 30min. Show 'Transcription in progress...' status in RecordingListView. On failure: show error with option to retry or fall back to local transcription. Design doc: docs/audio-transcription-design.md §5 Upload Flow

title: Fetch today's sessions from Pablo backend
description: Implement GET /sessions?date=today in APIClient. Returns [Session] decoded from JSON. Called by SessionListViewModel on app launch and on pull-to-refresh. Handle offline gracefully (show cached data or empty state with error message).

title: Google Meet browser launch
description: Google Meet has no native URL scheme. Launch via NSWorkspace.shared.open() with the https://meet.google.com/ URL in the default browser. Build meeting URL from session metadata.

title: Onboarding Step 4: Video platform detection + configuration
description: Build OnboardingVideoView. Detect installed apps (Zoom, Teams, Meet). Let therapist confirm their preferred platform. Store in @AppStorage. Pablo Bear copy: 'Which video platform do you use for sessions?'. Skippable — can configure later in Settings.

title: Set up CI (GitHub Actions — build + test on push)
description: Create .github/workflows/mac.yml triggering on mac/** changes. Run xcodebuild build and test. Create .github/workflows/core.yml triggering on core/** changes, running cargo build and cargo test.

title: Add AudioCaptureKit as Swift package dependency
description: Based on audit (PABLO-D-010), add AudioCaptureKit to the Xcode project — either as an SPM package pointing to pablo-health/audiotake2 or as a git submodule. Configure any required entitlements (microphone, screen recording) in Info.plist.

title: Detect installed video apps and show only relevant options
description: On first launch and in Settings, detect which video platforms are installed (Zoom: check zoommtg:// scheme; Teams: check msteams:// scheme; Meet: always available). Store preference in @AppStorage. Only show relevant launch options in the session UI.

title: Initialize Xcode project (SwiftUI, macOS target, SPM)
description: Create the Xcode project under mac/ with SwiftUI app template, macOS 14+ deployment target, Swift 6.0, and Swift Package Manager configured. No storyboards.

title: Audit audiotake2 repo — identify public API surface
description: Explore pablo-health/audiotake2 to document: (1) AudioCaptureKit public Swift API, (2) existing Pablo branding assets so we don't duplicate them, (3) macOS deployment target, (4) how auth is handled, (5) whether it's suitable as an SPM package or needs submodule. Output a short audit doc.

title: Epic 2: AudioCaptureKit Integration
Epic 4: Backend Sync
Epic 3: Core Session UI
Epic 1: Project Foundation
Verify recording start/stop in sandbox view
description: Create a temporary debug view with Start/Stop buttons that exercises RecordingService. Confirm audio captures and file is written to disk. No UI polish required — this is a smoke test. Remove or gate behind #DEBUG before shipping.

title: Guard DemoEncryptor behind DEBUG flag and implement production key generation
description:
## Problem
DemoEncryptor.swift uses a hardcoded sequential key (0x01…0x20) committed to a public repo. Any recording 'encrypted' with this key is effectively plaintext — a HIPAA violation for a production app.

## Revised Approach (approved 2026-02-25)
Delete DemoEncryptor entirely. Replace with a single persistent device key stored in Keychain.

- Generate one AES-256-GCM key per device on first launch via SecRandomCopyBytes()
- Store in Keychain with kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly
- Use for all recordings on this device
- Decrypt in memory before upload (key never leaves device); backend receives plaintext over HTTPS
- Add 'Delete after upload' setting (defaults ON) in Settings

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DemoEncryptor.swift deleted
- [ ] RecordingViewModel generates device key on first launch via SecRandomCopyBytes() if Keychain miss
- [ ] Key stored in Keychain with kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly
- [ ] Recordings encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM using device key
- [ ] Recordings decrypted in memory before upload; key never transmitted
- [ ] Settings: 'After upload' toggle — 'Delete from device' (default ON) / 'Keep on device'
- [ ] RecordingViewModel encryption toggle removed from SettingsView in all builds (no Debug mode toggle)
- [ ] Xcode build zero warnings

## Files
- mac/PabloCompanion/Services/DemoEncryptor.swift (DELETE)
- mac/PabloCompanion/ViewModels/RecordingViewModel.swift
- mac/PabloCompanion/Views/SettingsView.swift
- mac/PabloCompanion/Services/KeychainManager.swift (add device key methods)

title: Enforce HTTPS-only for all backend and auth server URLs
description:
## Problem
Multiple ViewModels default to `http://localhost:8000` and accept arbitrary HTTP URLs from user input. CodeQL flags this as `swift/cleartext-transmission`. Transmitting auth tokens and patient data over HTTP is a HIPAA violation.

## Affected Files
- `mac/PabloCompanion/Services/APIClient.swift` (line 15)
- `mac/PabloCompanion/ViewModels/AuthViewModel.swift` (buildAuthURL)
- `mac/PabloCompanion/ViewModels/UploadViewModel.swift` (line 8)
- `mac/PabloCompanion/ViewModels/PatientViewModel.swift` (line 15)
- `mac/PabloCompanion/Views/SettingsView.swift` (auth server URL field)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All URL construction validates scheme is `https` before making any network call
- HTTP is only permitted in DEBUG builds (localhost dev server)
- Auth server URL text field shows validation error if user enters non-HTTPS URL
- Production default URLs point to `https://` endpoints
- Resolves open CodeQL alert #12 (swift/cleartext-transmission)

title: Move Firebase API key from UserDefaults to Keychain; remove from Settings UI
description:
## Problem
The Firebase Web API key is stored in `@AppStorage("firebaseAPIKey")` (UserDefaults, plaintext) and exposed as an editable TextField in SettingsView. UserDefaults is unencrypted and readable by any process running as the same user.

The key is already fetched correctly from the Next.js `/api/config` endpoint on startup (`ContentView.swift:32-43`) — the issue is just that it's persisted to UserDefaults instead of Keychain, and unnecessarily surfaced in the Settings UI.

## Fix

On startup, after fetching from `/api/config`:
1. Write the key to Keychain (`KeychainManager`) instead of `@AppStorage`
2. On subsequent launches, read from Keychain first — only call `/api/config` if Keychain is empty

No backend changes needed. The Next.js `/api/config` endpoint already exists and works.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `@AppStorage("firebaseAPIKey")` removed from `AuthViewModel`
- [ ] Key written to Keychain after successful fetch from `/api/config`
- [ ] Key read from Keychain on launch; `/api/config` fetch only if Keychain miss
- [ ] Firebase API key TextField removed from `SettingsView`
- [ ] Key never appears in logs at any level
- [ ] Xcode build zero warnings

## Files
- `mac/PabloCompanion/ViewModels/AuthViewModel.swift`
- `mac/PabloCompanion/Views/ContentView.swift`
- `mac/PabloCompanion/Views/SettingsView.swift`
- `mac/PabloCompanion/Services/KeychainManager.swift`

title: Backend: add GET /api/config endpoint to deliver Firebase API key
description:
## Context

The macOS companion app (Pablo Companion) currently requires the Firebase Web API key to be manually entered by the therapist in Settings, where it is stored in UserDefaults (plaintext). This is a HIPAA security finding (PABLO-D-98). The fix requires the backend to deliver this key securely after authentication so the companion never needs to store it persistently.

**Important distinction:** The backend already uses the Firebase *Admin SDK* with Application Default Credentials (`firebase_init.py`) — this is a separate credential from the Firebase Web API key. The Web API key is what client apps use to call Firebase Auth REST endpoints (e.g. token refresh via `securetoken.googleapis.com`).

## What Needs to Be Built

Add a new authenticated endpoint:

```
GET /api/config
```

### Response Schema

```json
{
  "firebase_web_api_key": "AIzaSy..."
}
```

### Auth Requirement

Requires a valid Firebase ID token (`Authorization: Bearer <token>`). Use the existing `get_current_user_id` dependency — no MFA or BAA required, since this is called during the login flow before those checks complete.

### Implementation Notes

- Add `FIREBASE_WEB_API_KEY` to `Settings` in `backend/app/settings.py` (Pydantic `SecretStr` field, read from environment)
- Create `backend/app/routes/config.py` with the new router, following the existing pattern in `users.py` / `sessions.py`
- Register the router in `backend/app/main.py`
- The key is already present in the Firebase project — find it in the Firebase Console under Project Settings → General → Web API Key (same project used by `firebase_project_id`)
- Add the env var to Cloud Run / deployment secrets — do NOT commit the key

### Example Implementation

```python
# backend/app/routes/config.py
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
from ..auth.service import get_current_user_id
from ..settings import get_settings

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api", tags=["config"])

@router.get("/config")
def get_client_config(
    _user_id: str = Depends(get_current_user_id),
) -> dict:
    """Return client configuration. Requires authentication."""
    settings = get_settings()
    return {
        "firebase_web_api_key": settings.firebase_web_api_key.get_secret_value(),
    }
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/config` returns `{"firebase_web_api_key": "..."}` for authenticated requests
- [ ] Returns `401` for unauthenticated requests
- [ ] `FIREBASE_WEB_API_KEY` is read from environment — never hardcoded
- [ ] Env var added to Cloud Run secret config and documented in `DEVELOPMENT.md` / `.env.example`
- [ ] Endpoint appears in FastAPI `/docs`
- [ ] Unit test covers: authenticated success, unauthenticated 401
- [ ] No value logged at any log level

## Companion-Side Implementation

See PABLO-D-98. Once this endpoint ships, the companion fetches the key on session start, stores it in Keychain for the session lifetime only, and never writes it to UserDefaults.

title: Settings screen: re-access any onboarding step after setup
description: Build SettingsView accessible from the main UI. Sections: Account (re-login/logout), Permissions (mic, screen recording — link to System Settings), Video Platform (change default), Test Recording (run again). All steps from onboarding accessible here.

title: Implement RecordingService wrapper around AudioCaptureKit
description: Create mac/Sources/Services/RecordingService.swift — a @MainActor class wrapping AudioCaptureKit. Exposes async startRecording(sessionId:) and stopRecording() -> RecordingResult. Handles permission checks and publishes recording state.

title: Microsoft Teams deep link launch (msteams://)
description: Implement Teams launch in VideoLaunchService using msteams:// URL scheme. Same pattern as Zoom: check scheme availability, build meeting URL from session metadata, fall back to browser.

title: Session row component with status badge
description: Build reusable SessionRowView component. Shows: time slot, patient name, session status badge (scheduled/in-progress/completed). Badge colors follow branding: sage green for active/recording, honey for CTA states. Needs #Preview.

title: POST new session / patient to backend
description: Implement POST /sessions and POST /patients endpoints in APIClient. Called from the new session creation flow. Returns created Session/Patient with server-assigned ID. Handle validation errors and show user-friendly messages.

title: [epic] Epic 8: Windows Audio Capture
WASAPI device enumeration (default + loopback)
RecordingService (C# wrapper — start/stop/export)
Audio format handling (WAV/PCM → backend upload format)
Microphone + loopback permission prompts
Upload recording on session end
description: After session ends (RecordingService.stopRecording()), upload the audio file to Pablo backend. Use multipart upload. Associate with session ID. Show upload progress. Handle retry on failure. This triggers SOAP note generation server-side.

title: Onboarding flow architecture (step machine, resumable state)
description: Design and implement the onboarding step state machine. Stores current step in @AppStorage so it resumes after app restart. Steps: login → mic → screen-recording → video-platform → test-recording → ready. Allows skipping non-essential steps. Pablo Bear guides each step.

title: Onboarding Step 5: Test recording (5-second capture + playback)
description: Build OnboardingTestRecordingView. Press-to-record 5 seconds, then play back immediately so therapist confirms audio is working. Uses RecordingService. Shows waveform animation during capture. Pablo Bear celebrates success.

title: Onboarding Step 2: Microphone permission request + verification
description: Build OnboardingMicView. Request NSMicrophoneUsageDescription permission. Show current status and guide user to System Settings if denied. Pablo Bear copy: 'Pablo needs mic access to listen in sessions'. Verify permission is granted before advancing.

title: Empty state (no sessions today)
description: Design and implement the empty state view shown when there are no sessions scheduled for today. Should include Pablo Bear, warm copy ('No sessions today — enjoy the break'), and a button to create a new session. Follows brand guidelines.

title: Onboarding Step 6: 'You're ready' — Pablo Bear celebration + day view hand-off
description: Build OnboardingReadyView. Pablo Bear celebration moment — warm copy, maybe a subtle animation. 'You're all set\! Let's see today's sessions.' Button lands on the day view. Mark onboarding complete in @AppStorage.

title: Onboarding Step 1: Account connection / login screen
description: Build OnboardingLoginView. Options: enter Pablo API credentials or OAuth via browser. On success, store auth token in Keychain (following audiotake2 auth pattern). Show Pablo Bear with warm intro copy. Advance to mic step on success.

title: Onboarding Step 3: Screen recording / system audio permission
description: Build OnboardingScreenRecordingView. Required if AudioCaptureKit captures system audio. Check TCC permission status. Guide user to System Settings > Privacy > Screen Recording. Make skippable if only mic capture is needed (confirm in audiotake2 audit).

title: Set up MVVM folder structure
description: Create Models/ViewModels/Views/Services folder structure under mac/Sources/. Add .gitkeep files so the structure is committed. Matches architecture defined in CLAUDE.md.

title: [epic] Epic 11: Windows Onboarding
Onboarding flow architecture (step machine, resumable state — Windows)
Onboarding Step 1: Account connection / login screen
Onboarding Step 2: Microphone permission request + verification
Onboarding Step 3: Screen recording / system audio permission
Onboarding Step 4: Video platform detection + configuration
Onboarding Step 5: Test recording (5-second capture + playback)
Onboarding Step 6: 'You're ready' — Pablo Bear celebration + day view hand-off
Settings screen: re-access any onboarding step after setup
[epic] Epic 7: Windows Foundation
WinUI 3 project scaffold (AppSDK, MSIX packaging, hello-world window)
Rust → C# UniFFI bindings setup (pablo-core build + P/Invoke wiring)
Windows dev environment docs (Visual Studio + WinAppSDK prerequisites)
CI stub for windows/ path filter
CODEOWNERS + branch ruleset update for Windows required checks
Epic 5: Video Conferencing Launch
Zoom deep link launch (zoommtg://)
description: Implement Zoom launch in VideoLaunchService using NSWorkspace.shared.open() with zoommtg:// URL scheme. Build the meeting URL from session metadata. Verify scheme is registered (Zoom installed) before attempting launch — fall back to browser if not.

title: [epic] Epic 10: Windows Video Launch
Zoom deep link launch (zoommtg://) via ShellExecute
Microsoft Teams deep link launch (msteams://)
Google Meet browser fallback (ShellExecute default browser)
Detect installed video apps and show relevant options
